data.title: Target in charts: Q3 comparable sales -2.7% as digital growth fails to fully offset decline in store sales
Stock index futures inch up as investors look to Nvidia earnings
data.description:
Stock index futures pushed slightly higher Wednesday after a tough start to the week for equities.

Benchmark S&P 500 futures (SPX) advanced about half a percent, and so did Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND). Dow futures (INDU) were marginally up.

Wall Street is coming off its first four-day losing streak since August, as the artificial intelligence trade keeps taking it on the chin. After two days of risk-off selling on AI valuation and spending concerns, investors will likely continue to be cautious until Nvidia (NVDA) reports after the bell. 

Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid highlighted that Nvidia has now fallen more than 12% from its October 29 peak — its sharpest drop since the “Liberation Day” volatility earlier this year. He noted that even a major new AI deal failed to lift sentiment: Nvidia committed up to $10B to Anthropic and Microsoft up to $5B, with Anthropic set to buy $30B in Azure compute, yet both stocks declined on the day. Reid said "sentiment has turned more negative in the last few weeks, with the circular AI deals being treated with increasing caution as the conversation around a potential bubble has gathered pace."

data.title: GDS gives Q3 results, confirms guidance
data.description:
• GDS Holdings press release (GDS): Q3 GAAP EPADS of $0.45.
  • Revenue of $405.6M (-4.0% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Net revenue in the third quarter of 2025 was RMB2,887.1 million (US$405.6 million), a 10.2% increase over the same period last year of RMB2,619.6 million. The Y-o-Y increase was mainly due to continued ramp-up of our data centers.
  • Adjusted GP margin (non-GAAP) was 51.0% in the third quarter of 2025, compared with 50.5% in the same period last year.
  • Basic income per American Depositary Share (“ADS”) in the third quarter of 2025 was RMB3.70 (US$0.52), compared with basic loss of RMB1.12 in the same period last year.
  • The Company confirms that the previously provided guidance of total revenues for the year of 2025 of RMB11,290 – RMB11,590 million, Adjusted EBITDA of RMB5,190 – RMB5,390 million and the revised capex guidance of approximately RMB2,700 million remain unchanged

data.title: Target Non-GAAP EPS of $1.78 beats by $0.07, revenue of $25.3B in-line; updates FY outlook
data.description:
• Target press release (TGT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.78 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $25.3B (-1.4% Y/Y) in-line.

  • Guidance

    For the fourth quarter of 2025, the Company is maintaining its expectation of a low-single digit decline in sales.

    Full-year GAAP EPS is now expected to be approximately $7.70 to $8.70. Full-year Adjusted EPS, which excludes the gains from the litigation settlements in the first quarter and severance and asset-related charges in the third quarter, is now expected to be approximately $7.00 to $8.00 vs. consensus of $7.27.

data.title: LuxExperience B.V GAAP EPS of -€0.61, revenue of €573.5M; updates FY outlook
data.description:
• LuxExperience B.V press release (LUXE): Q1 GAAP EPS of -€0.61.
  • Revenue of €573.5M (+184.3% Y/Y).
  • GMV decline of -4.3% to €589.0 million in Q1 FY26 as compared to €615.3 million in the prior year period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of -€28.1 million with and Adjusted EBITDA margin of -5.0%.
  • Significantly increased Adjusted EBITDA profitability for Luxury | Mytheresa with an Adjusted EBITDA margin of 3.5%, up 210bps vs. Q1 FY25.
  • Outstanding GMV Growth for Luxury | Mytheresa of +13.5% vs. Q1 FY25 and Net Sales growth of +12.2% vs. Q1 FY25
  • Exceptional customer economics across all segments vs. Q1 FY25: Luxury | Mytheresa +15.0% increase in GMV per top customer; Luxury | NAP & MRP increase in GMV per top customer of +4.0%; Off-price | YOOX increase in GMV per top customer of +4.7%.

  • UPDATED GUIDANCE

    Therefore, LuxExperience now expects for FY26:

    • GMV €2.4 billion to €2.7 billion and
    • an Adjusted EBITDA margin between -2% to +1%

data.title: Lineage subsidiary proposes euro-denominated notes offering
data.description:
• Lineage’s (LINE) indirect subsidiary Lineage Europe Finco B.V. plans to offer euro-denominated senior notes in a private placement.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to repay amounts outstanding under its revolving credit facility and for other general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Lowe’s Q3 profit tops estimates; full-year revenue outlook exceeds expectations
South Korean antitrust regulators visit Arm’s office in Seoul: report
data.description:
Officials from South Korea’s antitrust regulator visited the Seoul offices of Arm (ARM) this week as part of ongoing scrutiny of the British chip designer's licensing practices, Bloomberg News reported, citing people  with knowledge of the matter.

The Korea Fair Trade Commission is carrying out the unannounced inspections of Arm as part of an inquiry into its conduct, the report added.

The move comes in part from a complaint by Qualcomm (QCOM)  alleging that Arm is hurting competition by restricting access to its technology after operating an open network for over 20 years, the report noted.

Arm and Qualcomm did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The American chipmaker has also brought its concerns about Arm’s licensing business to the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and European Commission, according to the report.

Qualcomm alleges that Arm formed a reliance on its technology via an open licensing model, which also fostered a thriving chip industry, the report noted.

Arm, which is majority-owned by SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF), sells chip designs and licenses a so-called instruction set, a code used by software to communicate with processors. Chipmakers like Qualcomm and device makers such as Apple (AAPL) rely on Arm licenses for their products.

Qualcomm is telling competition authorities globally that the dynamic market is now threatened as Arm curbs access to boost profits through its own chipmaking ambitions, Bloomberg previously reported. Arm previously said Qualcomm’s complaints to regulators look to “expand the parties’ ongoing commercial dispute for its own competitive benefit,” the report noted.

Both companies are trying to position themselves to ride a wave in computing demand, while the market for smartphone chips, which had fueled much of their growth in recent years, has become slow.

The two companies are in a legal tussle globally. Qualcomm largely prevailed in a trial late last year over claims by Arm that it breached its licensing agreement. Meanwhile, last month, Arm appealed a judge's ruling in a licensing dispute against Qualcomm that left the American company's jury victory intact.

Qualcomm’s complaints to regulators are confidential, but in court filings last year, the company noted that after Arm’s acquisition by Japan’s SoftBank and the failure of an attempted sale to Nvidia (NVDA), Arm is acting anticompetitively, the report added.

data.title: BlackRock declares $5.21 dividend
data.description:
• BlackRock (BLK) declares $5.21/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.

data.title: Global-E Online beats top-line estimates; gives Q4 and updates FY outlook
data.description:
• Global-E Online press release (GLBE): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.07 in-line.
  • Revenue of $220.78M (+25.5% Y/Y) beats by $2.92M.
  • GMV1 in the third quarter of 2025 was $1,512 million, an increase of 33% year over year.
  • Non-GAAP Gross Margin2 in the third quarter of 2025 was 46.3%, compared with 46.7% in the third quarter of 2024. GAAP Gross Margin in the third quarter of 2025 was 45.1%
  • Adjusted EBITDA3 in the third quarter of 2025 was $41.3 million compared to $31.1 million in the third quarter of 2024, an increase of 33% year over year.
  • Net cash from operating activities in the third quarter of 2025 was $74.3 million compared with $30.3 million in the third quarter of 2024
  • Free Cash Flow in the third quarter of 2025 was $73.6 million, compared with $29.9 million in the third quarter of 2024.

  • Q4 2025 and Full Year Outlook

    Global-e is introducing fourth quarter guidance and is raising the full year guidance as follows:

      Q4 2025   FY 2025   Previous FY 2025
    (in millions)
    GMV(1) $2,195 - $2,315   $6,404 - $6,524   $6,220 - $6,520
    Revenue $318.5 - $334.5 (consensus of $321.79M)   $944.1 - $960.1 (consensus of $945.61M)   $921.5 - $971.5
    Adjusted EBITDA(3) $74.3 - $88.7   $185.6 - $200.0   $180 - $200
